∑ Math Journey
Chapters

Math Journey

Mathematics as a human adventure

From the first counting marks pressed into clay to the geometry of curved space-time — every idea in mathematics grew from a real human need. This is where that story unfolds.

World 00

The Language of Number

12 chapters

Ch. 1

Why Numbers Exist

How quantity became something humans could hold, share, and trust

  • Understand why human communities needed numbers.
  • Distinguish counting from measurement.
35 min foundational

Ch. 2

How Ancient Peoples Counted

Before there were numerals, there were fingers, notches, and knots

  • Understand the principle of one-to-one correspondence
  • Recognize tally marks as one of the earliest counting systems
30 min foundational

Ch. 3

Numeral Systems Around the World

Every civilization invented its own way to write numbers — and each reveals something different

  • Read and write numbers using Egyptian hieroglyphic and Roman numeral systems
  • Explain why positional (place-value) systems are more powerful than additive systems
35 min foundational

Ch. 4

Place Value — Why Position Changes Everything

The same digit means something completely different depending on where it stands

  • Identify the place value of each digit in a multi-digit number
  • Write numbers in expanded form and standard form
35 min foundational

Ch. 5

Addition and Subtraction

The first two operations — combining and taking away

  • Add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using place value
  • Apply the commutative and associative properties of addition
35 min foundational

Ch. 6

Multiplication

Repeated addition, scaled up — and why it changed commerce forever

  • Understand multiplication as repeated addition and as an area model
  • Apply the commutative, associative, and distributive properties of multiplication
40 min foundational

Ch. 7

Division

Sharing equally — and what happens when things don't divide evenly

  • Understand division as equal sharing and as repeated subtraction
  • Identify dividend, divisor, quotient, and remainder
40 min foundational

Ch. 8

Factors, Multiples, and Divisibility

The hidden structure of whole numbers — who divides whom

  • Define factor and multiple and identify them for given numbers
  • Apply divisibility rules for 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, and 10
35 min foundational

Ch. 9

Prime Numbers — The Atoms of Arithmetic

The building blocks of every number — and one of mathematics' deepest mysteries

  • Define prime and composite numbers and identify them
  • Use the Sieve of Eratosthenes to find primes up to 100
40 min foundational

Ch. 10

Fractions — Parts of a Whole

What to do when things don't divide evenly — and why fractions are everywhere

  • Understand a fraction as part of a whole, part of a set, and as division
  • Generate equivalent fractions and simplify fractions to lowest terms
45 min foundational

Ch. 11

Decimals and the Metric System

Fractions in base ten — and the beautiful simplicity of the metric system

  • Read and write decimal numbers, identifying the value of each decimal place
  • Convert between fractions and decimals
40 min foundational

Ch. 12

Estimation and Number Sense

Knowing when an answer is reasonable — the mathematician's most underrated skill

  • Round numbers to estimate sums, differences, products, and quotients
  • Use front-end estimation and compatible numbers strategies
35 min foundational

World 01

Shape, Measure, and the Greek Project

14 chapters

Ch. 13

Measuring Land and Space

How ancient peoples turned shape and distance into manageable numbers

  • Understand why measurement systems developed in ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia
  • Distinguish between length, perimeter, and area
35 min foundational

Ch. 14

Early Practical Geometry

When shapes and angles became tools for building and surveying

  • Understand why the right angle was essential for construction and surveying
  • Recognize Pythagorean triples as empirical tools used before proof
35 min foundational

Ch. 15

The Greek Turn Toward Proof

Why asking 'why?' changed mathematics forever

  • Understand the difference between empirical rules and proven theorems
  • Recognize that the Greeks shifted from "does it work?" to "why must it be true?"
35 min foundational

Ch. 16

Euclid and the Axiomatic Method

How one book shaped 2,000 years of mathematics, science, and logic

  • Understand Euclid's five postulates and why they are foundational
  • Recognize the axiomatic method as a way to build knowledge from simple axioms
35 min foundational

Ch. 17

Points, Lines, and Angles

The basic objects of geometry, and how to measure direction and rotation

  • Understand points, lines, and rays as geometric primitives.
  • Classify angles by measure and type.
35 min foundational

Ch. 18

Triangles

The simplest polygon, the strongest shape, and the foundation of all geometry

  • Understand the triangle as the simplest polygon.
  • Classify triangles by sides and by angles.
35 min foundational

Ch. 19

Congruence and Similarity

When shapes are identical, proportional, and how to measure the world indirectly

  • Understand the difference between congruence and similarity.
  • Apply the congruence criteria SSS, SAS, ASA, and AAS to prove triangles are congruent.
35 min foundational

Ch. 20

Area

How to measure the space inside a shape

  • Understand what area measures and why it matters.
  • Calculate the area of rectangles, triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids.
35 min foundational

Ch. 21

The Pythagorean Theorem

The most important relationship in geometry—and its surprising age

  • State the Pythagorean theorem precisely.
  • Understand why the theorem is true through visual proof.
45 min foundational

Ch. 22

Circles and Arcs

Pi, rotation, and the most perfect shape

  • Understand what a circle is and its basic properties.
  • Use the formulas for circumference and area of a circle.
40 min foundational

Ch. 23

Polygons and Tessellations

When shapes fit perfectly, and why only some shapes do

  • Classify polygons by the number of sides and regularity of angles.
  • Use the angle sum formula to calculate interior angles of polygons.
35 min foundational

Ch. 24

Solids and Volume

How to measure the space a three-dimensional shape contains

  • Understand the difference between 2D shapes and 3D solids.
  • Calculate volume for rectangular prisms, cylinders, cones, and spheres.
35 min foundational

Ch. 25

Geometric Construction

What can be built with only compass and straightedge—and what cannot

  • Perform basic compass-and-straightedge constructions.
  • Understand the rules and constraints of classical construction.
35 min foundational

Ch. 26

What Proof Is

Why one example is never enough, and how we know something is always true

  • Understand the difference between an example and a proof.
  • Distinguish between direct proof and proof by contradiction.
40 min foundational

World 02

Algebra Is Born

14 chapters

Ch. 27

The Problem of the Unknown

Why humans needed a symbol for what they didn't yet know

  • Understand why the concept of an unknown quantity emerged in ancient mathematics
  • Recognize that algebra arose from practical problems before it became symbolic
35 min foundational

Ch. 28

Zero and Negative Numbers

The numbers that shouldn't exist — and changed everything

  • Understand why zero was a controversial and revolutionary invention
  • Explain what negative numbers represent and why they were resisted
40 min foundational

Ch. 29

Al-Khwarizmi and the Birth of Algebra

How one scholar's book gave us the word 'algebra' — and a new way to think

  • Understand the historical context of al-Khwarizmi's work in Baghdad
  • Recognize the meaning of "algebra" (al-jabr) and "algorithm" as derived from this period
40 min foundational

Ch. 30

From Words to Symbols

How mathematics learned to speak in shorthand

  • Understand why mathematical notation developed gradually over centuries
  • Recognize the contributions of Viète and Descartes to modern symbolic algebra
35 min foundational

Ch. 31

Variables and Expressions

What it means to name the unknown — and how to compute with it

  • Define variable, expression, term, coefficient, and constant
  • Write algebraic expressions from word descriptions
35 min foundational

Ch. 32

The Equation and the Balance

An equation is a promise of equality — and breaking it changes everything

  • Understand an equation as a statement that two expressions are equal
  • Use the balance model to understand why the same operation must be applied to both sides
35 min foundational

Ch. 33

Solving Linear Equations

How to isolate the unknown — the core skill of algebra

  • Solve one-step linear equations using inverse operations
  • Solve two-step and multi-step linear equations
40 min foundational

Ch. 34

Ratio, Proportion, and Percent

Comparing quantities — the mathematics of scaling and fairness

  • Define ratio and write ratios in multiple forms
  • Set up and solve proportions using cross-multiplication
40 min foundational

Ch. 35

Word Problems and Mathematical Modeling

Translating the messy world into clean mathematics — and back again

  • Define variables to represent unknown quantities in a problem
  • Translate a word problem into an algebraic equation
40 min foundational

Ch. 36

Systems of Two Equations

Two unknowns, two conditions — how to pin down both at once

  • Understand why one equation with two unknowns has infinitely many solutions
  • Solve systems of two linear equations using substitution
45 min foundational

Ch. 37

Quadratic Expressions and Factoring

When the unknown is squared — a new kind of equation appears

  • Identify and write quadratic expressions in standard form $ax^2 + bx + c$
  • Expand products of two binomials using FOIL/distribution
45 min foundational

Ch. 38

The Quadratic Formula

A formula for every quadratic — and the secret of the discriminant

  • Complete the square to transform a quadratic into vertex form
  • Derive and apply the quadratic formula $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$
45 min foundational

Ch. 39

Patterns and Sequences

When numbers follow a rule — and how to find what comes next

  • Identify arithmetic and geometric sequences
  • Find the common difference or common ratio
40 min foundational

Ch. 40

What Algebra Opened Up

From one unknown to the language of the universe

  • Understand that algebra is the language through which modern science is expressed
  • Recognize that the methods learned in this world extend to polynomials, functions, and calculus
40 min foundational

World 03

Coordinate Space and Functions

12 chapters

Ch. 41

The Coordinate Plane

Descartes in bed, watching a fly — and the invention that unified algebra and geometry

  • Plot and identify points on a coordinate plane
  • Apply the distance and midpoint formulas
35 min foundational

Ch. 42

Graphing Linear Equations

Every straight line hides an equation — and every equation draws a line

  • Graph a linear equation using a table of values
  • Identify x- and y-intercepts and use them to graph
35 min foundational

Ch. 43

Slope — The Measure of Steepness

Rise over run — the single number that captures a line's character

  • Calculate slope from two points or a graph
  • Interpret slope as a rate of change
35 min foundational

Ch. 44

Linear Functions and Their Equations

Three ways to write a line — and why each form tells you something different

  • Write linear equations in slope-intercept, point-slope, and standard form
  • Convert between forms
40 min foundational

Ch. 45

What Is a Function?

One input, exactly one output — the most important idea in all of mathematics

  • Define a function and distinguish functions from non-functions
  • Identify domain and range
35 min core

Ch. 46

Quadratic Functions and Parabolas

Why thrown objects trace a perfect curve — and the mathematics behind it

  • Identify and graph quadratic functions
  • Find the vertex and axis of symmetry
40 min core

Ch. 47

Exponential Functions — Growth and Decay

When things double and halve — the mathematics of pandemics, savings, and radioactivity

  • Identify and evaluate exponential functions
  • Distinguish growth from decay
40 min core

Ch. 48

Logarithms — The Inverse of Exponential

The operation that turns multiplication into addition — and made astronomy possible

  • Convert between exponential and logarithmic form
  • Apply logarithm properties
40 min core

Ch. 49

Transformations of Functions

Shift, reflect, stretch — how one function becomes a whole family

  • Apply vertical and horizontal shifts to function graphs
  • Reflect functions across axes
35 min core

Ch. 50

Inverse Functions

Undoing what a function does — and the mathematics of decoding

  • Find the inverse of a function algebraically
  • Apply the horizontal line test
35 min core

Ch. 51

Systems of Equations — A Graphical View

Where two lines meet — and what it means when they don't

  • Solve systems graphically by finding intersection points
  • Identify consistent, inconsistent, and dependent systems
35 min core

Ch. 52

Modeling with Functions

Choosing the right mathematical tool — and reading what the model tells you

  • Choose an appropriate function type to model data
  • Build linear, quadratic, and exponential models
40 min core

World 04

Trigonometry, Astronomy, and the Heavens

12 chapters

Ch. 53

Angles and the Language of Turning

Degrees, radians, and why a circle has 360 degrees — and also 2π

  • Convert between degrees and radians
  • Find arc length using s = rθ
35 min core

Ch. 54

The Unit Circle — Sine and Cosine

A circle of radius one — and the two functions that describe all of wave mathematics

  • Define sine and cosine using the unit circle
  • Recall exact values at key angles
40 min core

Ch. 55

The Six Trigonometric Functions

Tangent, secant, and the ratios that solve every triangle

  • Define all six trig functions using right triangles
  • Use SOH-CAH-TOA to solve right triangles
40 min core

Ch. 56

Graphs of Sine and Cosine

The wave that runs through sound, light, tides, and heartbeats

  • Identify amplitude, period, phase shift, and vertical shift from an equation
  • Graph transformations of sine and cosine
40 min core

Ch. 61

Inverse Trigonometric Functions

Working backwards from a ratio to an angle — the tool every engineer uses

  • Foundational lesson
35 min core

Ch. 62

Vectors — Magnitude and Direction

Quantities that have both size and direction — the language of force and motion

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 63

Waves, Oscillation, and the Music of Mathematics

How sine and cosine describe every wave in the universe

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 64

What Trigonometry Opened Up

From measuring triangles to describing the universe — the reach of an angle

  • Foundational lesson
35 min core

Ch. ?

Polar Coordinates and Complex Plane

Another way to describe every point — and why circles become simple

  • Plot and identify points in polar coordinates
  • Convert between polar and Cartesian forms
40 min core

Ch. ?

The Law of Sines and the Law of Cosines

Solving any triangle — the tools that let navigators cross oceans

  • Apply the Law of Sines to solve triangles in AAS, ASA, and SSA cases
  • Apply the Law of Cosines in SAS and SSS cases
40 min core

Ch. ?

Trigonometric Identities

Equations that are always true — and the art of transforming expressions

  • Apply Pythagorean, reciprocal, and quotient identities
  • Use sum, difference, and double-angle formulas
40 min core

Ch. ?

Trigonometry and Ancient Astronomy

How the Greeks measured the sky — and the mathematics that let them

  • Explain Eratosthenes' method for measuring Earth's circumference
  • Apply angular measurement to estimate astronomical distances
35 min core

World 05

Change, Motion, and Calculus

12 chapters

Ch. 65

The Problem of Instantaneous Change

How fast is something moving right now? — the question that created calculus

  • Compute average rates of change from tables and equations
  • Interpret average rate of change as a slope
35 min core

Ch. 66

Limits — The Foundation of Calculus

Approaching without arriving — the rigorous idea beneath all of calculus

  • Evaluate limits using substitution, factoring, and limit laws
  • Distinguish one-sided limits
40 min core

Ch. 67

The Derivative — Measuring Instantaneous Change

The slope of a curve at a single point — and how to find it exactly

  • Compute derivatives using the limit definition
  • Interpret the derivative as slope of tangent and instantaneous rate of change
40 min core

Ch. 68

Differentiation Rules

Shortcuts that save the world — the rules that make calculus practical

  • Apply power, product, quotient, and chain rules
  • Differentiate trig, exponential, and logarithmic functions
40 min core

Ch. 73

ch-073-integration-techniques

The art of antidifferentiation — strategies for integrals that resist direct rules

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 74

ch-074-differential-equations

Equations that describe how things change — and the laws they reveal

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 75

ch-075-sequences-and-series

What happens when you add infinitely many terms? — sometimes a finite answer

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 76

ch-076-what-calculus-opened-up

From Newton's apple to quantum mechanics — the science calculus made possible

  • Foundational lesson
35 min core

Ch. ?

Applications of Derivatives

Finding highest peaks and lowest valleys — the mathematics of optimisation

  • Find critical points and classify them
  • Apply first and second derivative tests
40 min core

Ch. ?

Applications of Integration

Area between curves, accumulated distance, and the volume of a solid

  • Find area between two curves
  • Distinguish net displacement from total distance
40 min core

Ch. ?

The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus

The theorem that unites differentiation and integration — the heart of calculus

  • State both parts of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
  • Find antiderivatives using basic rules
40 min core

Ch. ?

The Integral — Measuring Accumulation

The area under a curve — and how to add up infinitely many infinitely thin slices

  • Estimate areas using left, right, and midpoint Riemann sums
  • Set up definite integrals
40 min core

World 06

Probability, Statistics, and Uncertainty

12 chapters

Ch. 81

The Binomial Distribution

Counting successes in repeated trials — from coin flips to clinical trials

  • Identify binomial experiments
  • Apply the binomial probability formula
40 min core

Ch. 82

The Normal Distribution

The bell curve — why so many things in nature cluster around the middle

  • Describe properties of the normal distribution
  • Apply the 68-95-99.7 rule
40 min core

Ch. 83

Describing Data — Centre, Spread, and Shape

How to summarise a thousand numbers in five — the art of statistics

  • Compute mean, median, and mode
  • Calculate variance, standard deviation, and IQR
35 min core

Ch. 84

Correlation and Linear Regression

When two variables move together — and how to find the line that fits best

  • Describe association from a scatter plot
  • Interpret the correlation coefficient r
40 min core

Ch. 85

Sampling and Statistical Inference

How a sample of 1,000 can tell you about 300 million — if done carefully

  • Distinguish population parameters from sample statistics
  • Identify sampling methods and potential biases
40 min core

Ch. 86

The Central Limit Theorem — Why Normality Appears Everywhere

The theorem that explains why the bell curve is everywhere — and why statistics works

  • State the Central Limit Theorem
  • Compute the mean and standard error of the sampling distribution
40 min core

Ch. 87

Bayes' Theorem — Updating Beliefs with Evidence

How to change your mind rationally — the mathematics of learning from experience

  • State and apply Bayes' Theorem
  • Distinguish prior and posterior probability
40 min core

Ch. 88

What Statistics Opened Up

From gambling tables to the foundations of modern science — the reach of uncertainty

  • Reflect on the arc of World 06
  • Connect statistics to medicine, science, and AI
35 min core

Ch. ?

Conditional Probability and Independence

How new information changes what we should believe

  • Compute conditional probabilities
  • Identify independent events
40 min core

Ch. ?

Counting — Permutations and Combinations

How many ways? — the art of counting without listing everything

  • Apply the fundamental counting principle
  • Compute permutations and combinations
35 min core

Ch. ?

Random Variables and Expected Value

Assigning numbers to chance — and the long-run average of any game

  • Define and use discrete random variables
  • Construct probability distributions
40 min core

Ch. ?

The Mathematics of Chance

How gambling dens gave birth to one of mathematics' most important branches

  • Define sample space and event
  • Compute probability of simple events
35 min core

World 07

Fields, Waves, Space-Time, and the Modern View

11 chapters

Ch. 89

Complex Numbers — The Imaginary Made Real

The square root of negative one — the number that shouldn't exist, but does everything

  • Perform arithmetic with complex numbers
  • Plot complex numbers on the Argand diagram
40 min core

Ch. 90

Vectors and Matrices

Arrays of numbers that encode transformations — the mathematics of graphics, AI, and physics

  • Perform matrix addition, scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication
  • Compute 2×2 determinants and inverses
40 min core

Ch. 91

Non-Euclidean Geometry — When Parallel Lines Meet

What happens if Euclid's fifth postulate is false — and why Einstein needed it

  • State Euclid's parallel postulate and explain why it is independent of the other four
  • Describe the properties of spherical and hyperbolic geometries
40 min core

Ch. 92

The Mathematics of Symmetry — Groups

The abstract structure beneath every pattern — and the mathematics that unifies physics

  • Identify symmetry groups of geometric figures
  • Verify group axioms for simple examples
40 min core

Ch. 93

Graph Theory and Networks

The bridges of Königsberg, the internet, and the mathematics of connection

  • Foundational lesson
35 min core

Ch. 94

Information Theory — The Mathematics of Communication

Claude Shannon and the bit — how mathematics learned to measure surprise

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 95

Fourier Analysis — Decomposing Signals

Any wave is a sum of sine waves — the most powerful idea in applied mathematics

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 96

Special Relativity — Space, Time, and the Speed of Light

What happens when nothing can travel faster than light — the mathematics of Einstein's revolution

  • Foundational lesson
40 min core

Ch. 97

Quantum Mechanics and Probability

The universe is not deterministic — it is fundamentally probabilistic

  • Describe the role of probability in quantum mechanics
  • State the uncertainty principle and its implications
40 min core

Ch. 98

Gödel's Incompleteness — The Limits of Mathematics

The theorem that proved mathematics cannot prove everything

  • Explain the goals of Hilbert's programme
  • State Gödel's First and Second Incompleteness Theorems
40 min core

Ch. 99

Chaos Theory and Dynamical Systems

Tiny changes, wildly different outcomes — why the future cannot always be predicted

  • Describe sensitive dependence on initial conditions
  • Apply the logistic map and trace period doubling
40 min core
